finding: Slovak firms face margin compression as cost expectations outpace selling prices
bullets: Slovak firms (n=224) anticipated non-labour input price rises of 7.33% over the next 12 months, against selling price expectations of only 3.75% (n=222) — squeezing margins further.
Wage expectations remained at 4.12% (n=233) — easing just 0.09 pp from the prior wave — outpacing the EA's 2.60% by 1.52 pp and sustaining margin compression pressure.
Slovak 12-month inflation expectations eased 0.29 pp to 4.68% (n=193), remaining 0.80 pp above the EA average of 3.88% — adding to cost pressures.
chart_subtitle: Slovak input price expectations at 7.33%, far above selling prices.
